title: FAQs | Expert Witness & Education Psychology Services
description: Answers to common questions about expert witness instruction, educational psychology, costs, timelines, and how Psychology Direct works.
keywords: how much does expert witness cost
how long does expert report take

blocks.headline: Answers to Your Expert Witness & Education Questions
blocks.subtitle: Quick answers to your questions about instruction, costs, and timelines. Can't find your answer? Call us on 01306 879 975.

tabs.label: Legal Experts
tabs.content:
**How quickly can I get CVs for an expert witness?**

We typically provide 2–3 CVs and quotes within 24 hours of your brief. For urgent cases, we respond faster. Each CV includes qualifications, relevant experience, and the fee breakdown required by the Legal Aid Agency.

**Do your experts work at Legal Aid rates?**

Yes, all our experts work at Legal Aid rates for publicly funded cases. We provide full fee breakdowns with every quote. For privately funded cases, we provide transparent quotes upfront with the detailed hourly breakdown the LAA requires.

**How much does an expert witness cost?**

Standard reports typically range from Ł3,200–Ł5,500 depending on expertise, complexity, and timeline. Psychiatric assessments are typically Ł4,000–Ł7,000. Fast-track reports cost more. Use our Fee Calculator to estimate costs.

**What’s included in the fee?**

Our fees cover assessment, report writing, 100% quality assurance, administrative coordination, and client management. You receive a breakdown of hours spent on each component as required by the LAA.

**How long will it take to get the report?**

Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks from assessment. We offer fast-track (2–3 weeks) and urgent options at premium rates. Timelines vary by expert availability and case complexity.

**Can you work within court timelines and statutory deadlines?**

Yes. We understand family proceedings timelines, criminal disclosure deadlines, and statutory requirements. We regularly deliver within these frameworks.

**Do I need a psychologist or a psychiatrist for my case?**

It depends on what the court needs. A psychologist assesses behaviour, parenting capacity, and psychological functioning. A psychiatrist provides medical diagnosis of mental health conditions. Many cases benefit from both. Use our free tool at /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

**Can you handle multi-party assessments with complex logistics?**

Yes. Our Client Managers are experienced coordinating multi-party assessments across locations. We handle all booking, liaison, and administration so you focus on your case.

**How do you ensure the quality of expert reports?**

Every report is 100% proofread and quality-checked before reaching you. We verify instructions are addressed, recommendations are clear, and reports meet court standards. We vet all experts before registration, reviewing sample reports, verifying HCPC/GMC registration, and checking enhanced DBS status.

**What’s the process for instructing an expert?**

(1) Contact us with your case brief. (2) Client Manager takes a detailed brief. (3) We provide 2–3 CVs and quotes within 24 hours. (4) You instruct. (5) We manage all logistics. (6) Expert completes assessment. (7) We quality-check the report. (8) You receive the final report. Learn more: /about/how-it-works/

**Do I have to work through a Client Manager, or can I contact the expert directly?**

Your Client Manager is your primary point of contact and is fully briefed. They handle all queries, administration, and coordination. In rare circumstances where direct contact is appropriate, we can arrange it. This approach protects you and the expert and ensures consistent communication.

**What if I’m unsure whether my case needs an expert witness?**

Use our free Suitability Checker: /tools/suitability-checker/. Or call us on 01306 879 975. Our Client Managers have 15+ years’ experience and can advise at no cost.

**Are all your experts HCPC/GMC registered?**

Yes. All psychologists are HCPC-registered. All psychiatrists are GMC-registered. We verify enhanced DBS checking, professional indemnity insurance, and current CPD. We review expert reports before registration.

**Do you accept Legal Aid funding?**

Yes. All experts work at Legal Aid rates. We provide CVs and quotes in LAA format. We’re fully compliant with Legal Aid billing and payments.

tabs.label: Education Services
tabs.content:
**What is an Educational Psychologist?**

EPs are HCPC-registered professionals with doctoral training in psychology and education. They assess children’s learning, behaviour, and development in schools. They help identify special educational needs, assess for EHCP, and recommend interventions.

**When should we commission an EP assessment?**

Consider an EP assessment when: a child has learning difficulties or developmental delays; behaviour is concerning; you’re considering EHCP; or you’re seeking exam access arrangements. An EP can help identify what support is needed.

**How quickly can you find an Educational Psychologist?**

We provide CVs and quotes within 24 hours of your brief. Assessment usually begins within 1–2 weeks depending on EP availability and urgency.

**What does an EP assessment cost?**

Assessments typically range from Ł800–Ł2,500 depending on complexity and type. EHCP statutory assessments may be higher. Many LAs and schools have service contracts with discounted rates. Contact us for a quote.

**How long does an EP assessment take?**

Assessment usually takes 2–4 hours. Reports typically return within 2–4 weeks depending on EP workload. We aim for faster turnaround, especially for statutory EHCP assessments.

**Will our LA accept reports from your EPs?**

Yes. All our EPs are HCPC-registered and meet LA professional standards. Many LAs have worked with our EPs and accept their reports. We can provide LA references.

**Can we have the same EP for all our assessments?**

Yes, if that EP has capacity and relationship continuity is appropriate. Consistency can be valuable. Let us know and we’ll match the same EP for ongoing work.

**Do your EPs offer services beyond assessments?**

Yes. Our EPs provide consultancy, classroom strategy development, staff training, SENCO supervision, and intervention planning. Discuss your needs: /contact/

**How do we know your EPs are qualified and of good quality?**

All EPs are HCPC-registered, enhanced DBS-checked, and actively practicing. We review qualifications and track record before registration. Many have specialist training. We can provide references.

**How do you ensure the quality of EP reports?**

Every report is proofread and quality-checked before delivery. We verify instructions are addressed, recommendations are clear, and reports meet LA and EHCP standards. We gather feedback from schools and LAs.

**What happens after the EP assessment?**

The EP provides a detailed report with findings and recommendations. It informs EHCP decisions, support planning, or intervention design. The EP can attend meetings if needed. Follow-up support and implementation help are available.

**Can an EP report be used for exam access arrangements?**

Yes. EP psychometric assessment is used to evidence exam access arrangements like extra time or a reader. The EP conducts standardised tests and provides JCQ-compliant reports.

**How is Educational Psychology different from Clinical Psychology?**

EPs work with children’s learning, development, and educational needs in schools. Clinical Psychologists assess psychological and mental health issues broadly. For school-based SEND and learning support, an EP is typically right.

tabs.label: General
tabs.content:
**How does Psychology Direct work?**

We’re a managed service connecting organisations with vetted experts. A dedicated Client Manager takes your requirements, matches you with appropriate experts, and manages everything.

**What organisations do you work with?**

We work with solicitors and law firms, Local Authorities, schools and academies, multi-academy trusts, and government bodies. We don’t provide direct services to private individuals seeking personal therapy.

**Where are you located?**

We’re a fully remote national team. We have 1,000+ experts nationwide, so we can always find someone near your location. Availability is not geographically limited.

**Do you provide services across the whole UK?**

Yes. We work with experts nationwide: England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. Wherever your case or assessment needs to take place, we can find a local expert.

**How much does it cost to use your service?**

We don’t add a markup to expert fees. You pay the same rates as instructing directly, but benefit from our matching, vetting, and client management. Legal Aid rates are fixed LAA rates. Use our Fee Calculator for estimates.

**Do you charge a fee for getting quotes or expert CVs?**

No. There’s no charge for assessment or quotes. You only pay if you formally instruct an expert.

**What if I’m not sure which service I need?**

That’s what we’re here for. Call us on 01306 879 975 or use our free tools: Suitability Checker and Psychologist or Psychiatrist? tool.

**How do I contact you?**

Phone: 01306 879 975 | Email: enquiries@psychologydirect.co.uk | Contact form: /contact/ | Business hours Monday–Friday.

**What if I have an urgent case?**

We can often respond to urgent cases within hours. Call us immediately on 01306 879 975 to discuss your urgency and what’s possible. We have a dedicated urgent case protocol.

**Can I arrange payment on account?**

Yes, depending on your organization and contract terms. This is typically available for Local Authorities and regular legal clients. Discuss with your Client Manager or contact sales: /contact/

**Do I have to sign a contract?**

Standard instruction is by instruction letter. For ongoing arrangements or large contracts, we provide terms. Most single cases don’t require formal contracts.

**What data protection measures do you have in place?**

We take data protection seriously. All data complies with GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018. We use secure, encrypted communication and storage. Case details stay confidential and are shared only on a need-to-know basis.

**Can I speak to a Client Manager directly?**

Yes. Every client has a dedicated Client Manager. Once you instruct, they’re your single point of contact. Call 01306 879 975 during business hours.

blocks.text: Can't find your answer? Call us on 01306 879 975 or email enquiries@psychologydirect.co.uk. Our Client Managers are happy to help.

blocks.heading: Still Have Questions?
blocks.bodyText: Our FAQs cover common questions, but every case is unique. A Client Manager can discuss your situation and answer remaining questions at no cost.

blocks.heading: Ready to Instruct or Book an Assessment?
blocks.bodyText: If these FAQs answered your questions, our Client Managers can match you within 24 hours. CVs, quotes, and coordination included.
